name: LEMONGRASS CHILI TOFU
intro: There are two different ways to make this dish (đậu phụ sả ớt): you can coat deep-fried tofu with fried lemongrass and chili peppers, or you can stuff it with lemongrass and chili peppers before you deep-fry it. I prefer the former (below) for weeknight meals, as it’s much quicker and simpler to prepare. The latter method (see here) takes a bit more time and skill (especially for stuffing), but it’s totally worth the effort for special occasions or when you want to impress someone.

ingredients.item: vegetable oil, plus more for deep frying
bird’s eye chili pepper, sliced on the bias, for garnish
or 3 sprigs of cilantro, for garnish
vegetable oil, plus more for deep-frying
minced leek or scallion (white part only)
bird’s eye chili pepper, sliced, for garnish
or 3 sprigs of cilantro, for garnish

method: Fill the bottom of a small or medium saucepan with about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of oil and heat over low heat. After about 3 minutes, check with a cooking thermometer to see if oil has reached 320°F (160°C). (You can also dip the pointy end of a wooden chopstick in the oil, and if a steady stream of bubbles appears around it, the oil is ready.) Carefully drop in tofu cubes, cooking in batches if necessary, and fry until golden brown (about 8 minutes).
With a slotted spoon, transfer tofu to a paper towel–lined plate to drain.

In a medium skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over low heat. When hot, add lemongrass and chili pepper, and cook and stir until golden brown (1 to 2 minutes). Add stock powder, salt, sugar, and turmeric powder, and stir well.
Add fried tofu and drizzle with soy sauce. Toss until well-coated, and then remove from heat.
Arrange tofu on a platter, garnish with chili slices and cilantro, and serve with cucumber slices.

Cut tofu into 2 by 3-inch (5 by 7.5 cm) pieces that are ½ inch (1 cm) thick. Being careful not to cut all the way through, use a knife to slice into one side of each tofu piece and create space for the filling.
Stuff about 1 teaspoon of the filling into the created space of one of the tofu pieces. Repeat with the remaining tofu and filling.

Fill the bottom of a large skillet with about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of oil and heat over low heat. After about 3 minutes, check with a cooking thermometer to see if the oil has reached 320°F (160°C). (You can also dip the pointy end of a wooden chopstick in the oil, and if a steady stream of bubbles appears around it, the oil is ready.) Carefully drop in tofu and fry until golden brown on both sides (about 8 minutes). With a slotted spoon, transfer tofu to a paper towel–lined plate to drain.
Arrange tofu on a platter, garnish with chili slices and cilantro, and serve with cucumber slices and a bowl of soy sauce for dipping.
